title: classifyTool: toolType !== 'hand' is the only pass-through — selection is drawing mode
rationale: Wave 3b atlasdraw-dd91 option (a): isDrawingMode = toolType !== 'hand'. Selection tool now included in drawing set — geo-elements are clickable with selection. Hand is the sole map-pan tool. Prior decision mx-d75e98 had selection as pass-through because no geo-elements existed yet; Wave 3b's PinTool created elements that must be clickable. Trade-off: users must switch to hand tool explicitly to pan map (cannot click-drag while in selection mode). Codified in classifyTool.ts JSDoc; tools tests updated from 17→24 cases (+7 PinTool). Arrow/freedraw/polyline scaleMode:hybrid deferred to atlasdraw-375a.

title: classifyTool: only 'hand' is pass-through (supersedes selection-also-pass-through)
rationale: Wave 3a shipped classifyTool returning false for both 'hand' AND 'selection' per plan literal — meaning selection let pointerEvents fall through to MapLibre. Wave 3b prerequisite atlasdraw-dd91 surfaced the consequence: once PinTool ships, the user's first click on a placed pin (with selection tool active) would pan the map instead of selecting the pin. Phase 1 simplicity favored option (a): make selection drawing-mode (Excalidraw captures clicks) so geo-elements are selectable. Trade-off: hand tool (keyboard 'h' or hand button) is now the only way to pan the map; selection no longer doubles as map-pan. Phase 4 toolbar UX may revisit if dual-purpose selection is needed.

content: pre-dispatch integration-seam scrub: before dispatching N parallel workers that produce element shapes via a host bridge, grep the bridge accept-list and confirm coverage. Wave 1a scrub revealed seedToElement.ts:40 was Pin-only-throw and useAtlasdrawTool.ts:91 was a noisy stub — without the scrub, 8 parallel workers would have failed at runtime.

yarn workspace add lockfile race: yarn workspace <pkg> add <dep> prunes hoisted packages; after any yarn workspace add, immediately run bare 'yarn install' to re-hoist, then verify build before committing. Wave 1a: @turf/distance install pruned vite-plugin-checker; recovered via plain yarn install.

module-level singleton state for AtlasdrawTool: 6 of 7 Wave 1b tools (PolygonTool, FreehandTool, ArrowTool, RectangleTool, CircleTool, PolylineTool) use module-scope mutable state instead of closure-factory because the tool registry expects a statically-importable AtlasdrawTool object. Trade-off: single-pointer/single-active-tool invariant enforced. Revisit for Phase 7 plugin-worker port. PinTool and TextLabelTool (fire-and-forget) need no state. All 6 stateful tools document this trade-off in file headers.

tool hand-off boundary: when a tool wants to hand off control to another tool (e.g. TextLabelTool calling setActiveTool({type:'text'})), that is a host concern, not a tool concern. TextLabelTool emits an empty text element and defers inline-editing UX; atlasdraw-5193 tracks. Pattern confirmed Wave 1b (mx-682f8a).

vitest jsdom env for tools using DOM globals: tools-package vitest env was originally node; tools using KeyboardEvent (e.g. T04 PolylineTool onKeyDown) require jsdom. Workaround used in Wave 1b: cast via 'as unknown as KeyboardEvent'. Convention: if more tools need DOM globals, switch tools/vitest.config.ts environment to 'jsdom' (matches atlas-app W1a jsdom config).

title: multi-worker wave bundled into single commit
rationale: Wave 1a and Wave 1b each bundled all worker outputs into one commit (17 new files + 2 modified in Wave 1b). Rationale: workers are a parallel decomposition of one wave goal; vitest.config.ts is a shared artifact; audit trail for who-did-what lives in scrub doc + commit body, not in per-worker commits. Establishes precedent for future multi-worker waves.

title: tool test files colocated with source, not __tests__/
rationale: OQ-W1-4 resolved: tool tests live alongside source files (e.g. PolygonTool.test.ts next to PolygonTool.ts), matching the established PinTool convention. Avoids parallel-directory drift where test and source diverge in naming and ownership.

description: scrub draft confident-incorrect on absence — initial Wave 1b scrub claimed T05/T08 were clean based on regex token presence, missed implicit updateElement dependency in T08 drag preview. Expected: regex extraction confirms safety. Got: false negative; advisor caught the gap.
resolution: advisor review caught the miss; T08 brief was corrected before dispatch. Lesson: regex extracts confirm presence of patterns, never absence; pair with full-body read of sampled tasks.

name: wave1-pre-dispatch-scrub
description: Phase 2 Wave 1 pre-dispatch scrub canonical example: docs/decisions/wave1-pre-dispatch-scrub-2026-05-04.md (main + addendum). Integration seams verified: code/apps/atlas-app/src/tools/seedToElement.ts (8-branch bridge) + code/apps/atlas-app/src/hooks/useAtlasdrawTool.ts (updateElement impl). Preview pattern in docs/architecture/subsystems/tools/contracts.md.

name: wave1b-tool-implementations
description: Wave 1b tool reference implementations: code/packages/tools/src/{PolygonTool,PolylineTool,FreehandTool,TextLabelTool,ArrowTool,RectangleTool,CircleTool}.ts — 7 canonical impls shipped in commit 54e56f8. PolygonTool has the cleanest module-singleton trade-off documentation. GeoJSON parser T10 in code/packages/data/.

title: retro: Phase 2 Wave 1 — pre-dispatch scrub is load-bearing, not optional
rationale:
prediction_vs_reality: We expected 8 parallel workers could ship tools directly against the existing bridge; reality was the bridge had a Pin-only-throw at the accept-list and a stub updateElement — scrub caught both before a single worker was dispatched, saving 8 runtime failures.
key_decision_reversal: The decision to run a pre-dispatch integration-seam scrub for every multi-worker wave should be reversed only if the bridge becomes fully generic and test-covered at the accept-list boundary — at that point scrub collapses to a type-check.
feed_forward: Regex-only scrub produces false confidence on absence — T08 drag preview dependency was missed until full-body read; future scrubs must pair token-presence checks with sampled full-body reads of the most complex tasks.
trajectory: Wave 1a identified two integration seams (seedToElement accept-list, updateElement stub), extended both, added @turf deps (with a yarn hoisting detour recovered by bare install), then Wave 1b dispatched 7 tool workers + 1 data worker in parallel; all 8 landed cleanly in one bundled commit per wave, establishing the bundled-wave-commit precedent.
assumption_check: The original plan assumed the tools package vitest environment was sufficient for all tool tests; it is node-only and tools using DOM globals (KeyboardEvent) required cast workarounds — revisit vitest.config.ts environment before Phase 3 tools work.
